entities.name: SORGHUM BICOLOR
entities.description: The crop species for which a practical haplotype graph pangenome database was developed.

entities.name: PRACTICAL HAPLOTYPE GRAPH
entities.description: A pangenome database that stores haplotypes and variant information to facilitate imputation and genomic prediction.

entities.name: GENOME-WIDE VARIANTS
entities.description: Genetic variations across the genome identified using the PHG.

entities.name: SINGLE NUCLEOTIDE POLYMORPHISMS
entities.description: Specific single-base variations called by the PHG from sequence coverage.

entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding approach using genomic data to select superior genotypes.

entities.name: IMPUTATION
entities.description: The process of filling in missing genotypes using parental haplotypes from the PHG.

entities.name: GENOMIC PREDICTION
entities.description: The use of genotype data to predict the performance or traits of a cultivar.

entities.name: GENOTYPING-BY-SEQUENCING
entities.description: A genotyping method used as a comparison for prediction accuracies.

entities.name: TARGETED AMPLICON SEQUENCING
entities.description: A genotyping method (rhAmpSeq) used as a comparison for prediction accuracies.

entities.name: LOW-COVERAGE SEQUENCE DATA
entities.description: Sequencing data with low depth, such as 0.01x, from which SNPs are imputed.

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Organized efforts to accelerate the development of crop cultivars.

entities.name: CULTIVAR DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of creating and improving crop varieties.
